docs.text: How to use passGen to create 10-character passwords ¶ passGen is a lightweight Windows app for creating randomly generated, 10-character passwords that contain: Uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers Uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols Download passGen v2.0.0 For details about changes to the passGen app, see the release notes .
docs.title: Overview

docs.text: passGen is a lightweight Windows app for creating randomly generated, 10-character passwords that contain: Uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers Uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols Download passGen v2.0.0 For details about changes to the passGen app, see the release notes .
docs.title: How to use passGen to create 10-character passwords

docs.text: How to create a randomly generated, 10-character password ¶ After downloading passGen , double-click the executable file (.msi) to install the file on Windows. You can then choose which type of password to generate. Generate a password with symbols ¶ To create a 10-character password containing symbols , numbers , uppercase letters , and lowercase letters , click Create a password with symbols . Click Copy to copy the password. Example Generating a password with symbols Note To see the password that you generated, click the asterisks masking the password. Generate a password without symbols ¶ To create a 10-character password containing numbers , uppercase letters , and lowercase letters , click Create a password without symbols . Click Copy to copy the password. Example Generating a password without symbols Note To see the password that you generated, click the asterisks masking the password.
docs.title: Generating a password

docs.text: After downloading passGen , double-click the executable file (.msi) to install the file on Windows. You can then choose which type of password to generate.
docs.title: How to create a randomly generated, 10-character password

docs.text: To create a 10-character password containing symbols , numbers , uppercase letters , and lowercase letters , click Create a password with symbols . Click Copy to copy the password. Example Generating a password with symbols Note To see the password that you generated, click the asterisks masking the password.

docs.text: To create a 10-character password containing numbers , uppercase letters , and lowercase letters , click Create a password without symbols . Click Copy to copy the password. Example Generating a password without symbols Note To see the password that you generated, click the asterisks masking the password.
docs.title: Generate a password without symbols

docs.text: How to clear generated passwords ¶ To clear the passwords you generated, go to File and click Clear . Example Clearing generated passwords
docs.title: Clearing a password

docs.text: To clear the passwords you generated, go to File and click Clear . Example Clearing generated passwords
docs.title: How to clear generated passwords

docs.text: How to open a new window ¶ If you need to generate multiple passwords simultaneously, go to File and click Open new window to open another window. Example Opening a new window
docs.title: Opening a new window

docs.text: If you need to generate multiple passwords simultaneously, go to File and click Open new window to open another window. Example Opening a new window
docs.title: How to open a new window

docs.text: How to uninstall passGen ¶ To uninstall passGen, open Settings in Windows, then navigate to Add or remove programs . Search for passGen in the program list, and click the three vertical dots , then select Uninstall . Example Uninstalling passGen
docs.title: Uninstalling the app

docs.text: To uninstall passGen, open Settings in Windows, then navigate to Add or remove programs . Search for passGen in the program list, and click the three vertical dots , then select Uninstall . Example Uninstalling passGen
docs.title: How to uninstall passGen

docs.text: Contributions ¶ Did you encounter an error when using this app? Does this documentation need to be clarified, updated, or otherwise improved upon? If so, pull requests are welcome! Please do the following: Open an issue on GitHub and describe what you would like to add or change. Create a pull request and link to your issue. This will help me track changes to the documentation and help provide context for future reference.
